verify.rule: Ensure all required fields are present and non-empty.
Ensure the estimated mass matches the provided value.
Ensure P0 internal_feature_targets map to part_tree children and geometry_ops.
Ensure interfaces are reciprocal with neighboring subsystems.
Ensure critical joints have visible locking/preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion mismatch between aluminum and CFRP materials.
risk.mit: Use thermal interface materials and conductive paths to manage heat.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for moving parts during deployment.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearances based on actual component dimensions.

risk.desc: Risk of over-tightening bolts leading to structural damage.
risk.mit: Specify torque values and provide training on proper assembly techniques.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical shorts due to inadequate insulation.
risk.mit: Ensure all electrical connections are properly insulated and tested.

risk.desc: Risk of improper alignment during assembly due to lack of clear datum points.
risk.mit: Define clear datum points and provide visual aids for assembly.

physics_paths: Load_Path: Structural loads from solar panels and antennas transferred to frame via corner posts and rails.
Flow_Path: Electrical signals from onboard electronics routed through PCB standoffs.
Thermal_Path: Heat dissipation from electronic components to outer surfaces via aluminum fins.
Motion_Path: Deployable rails extend/retract along longitudinal axis.
Pressure_Path: No significant pressure differentials expected.
Human_Service_Access: Access panels for maintenance and inspection.

generated_parts.label: Solar Panel x 4 (deploy)
generated_parts.spec: Deployable solar panel wing, 3U size, spring-loaded hinge, burn-wire release, deployment damper

verify.rule: Ensure all required fields are present.
Ensure the subsystem's mass does not exceed the allocated budget.
Ensure each P0 feature maps to a specific geometry operation.
Ensure all interfaces are reciprocated by adjacent subsystems.
Ensure critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.desc: Spring-loaded hinge may not provide sufficient torque to overcome damper resistance.
risk.mit: Conduct mechanical testing to validate hinge performance.

risk.desc: Burn-wire release circuit may fail due to environmental factors.
risk.mit: Implement redundant circuit paths and conduct environmental testing.

risk.desc: Deployment damper may not effectively control panel speed during deployment.
risk.mit: Test damper performance under various conditions and adjust as necessary.

risk.desc: Seam between solar panel and hinge may leak during thermal cycling.
risk.mit: Apply a sealing compound to the seam and test for leaks.

risk.desc: Service access hole may interfere with adjacent subsystems.
risk.mit: Review and adjust placement of service access hole to avoid interference.

physics_paths.path: Deployable panel receives force from solar radiation and gravity, transferred through the hinge mechanism.
Panel rotates about the hinge axis due to spring tension and damper resistance.
Heat generated by solar radiation is dissipated through the aluminum structure and deployed panel.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum on the central axis of the hinge mechanism
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: ±0.1mm for machined parts, ±0.05mm for PCB components
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures will be oriented vertically for optimal chip removal
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Inspection via CMM for dimensional accuracy and visual inspection for surface finish

verification_focus: Mass budget check against 3U CubeSat structural requirements
Mechanical testing of the hinge mechanism under simulated solar radiation forces
Electrical integrity of the burn wire release circuit

generated_parts.label: Payload Optics Bench
generated_parts.spec: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently
